name: Content Volume & Game Length
definition: Reviews expressing that the game contains insufficient content relative to expectations, that the main story or overall runtime feels too short, or that content was artificially padded (fetch quests, repeated encounters) to inflate perceived length.
in_scope: Main story completion time described as too short; Claims of padding/filler to justify price; Comparison to expected DLC vs full-game content depth; Insufficient side content/activities; Lack of post-game or replayability
out_of_scope: Price complaints without content critique -> T02; Repetitive mission design as quality issue -> T09; Map size as physical space -> T03
sample_quotes: "The story is short DESPITE the fact that it is 75% filler junk added to pad the runtime in order to justify a 60$ price tag." [943 votes, 18h] | "After 25 hours in the game I can safely say it's not worth the price. Game is deceptively short." [453 votes, 25h]
classification_note: WARNING: Keyword match rate (6%) severely underestimates true prevalence (~25%). Reviewers express this theme indirectly: 'finished in a weekend', 'not enough for the price', 'DLC at full price'. Classifier prompts must include indirect expressions. Often co-occurs with T02 and T09.
keywords_indicative: too short, short game, padded, filler, not enough content, DLC-length, finished in a weekend, barely any content

name: Price-to-Value Perception
definition: Reviews where the central complaint is the perceived mismatch between the retail price ($60-$70) and what the game delivers. Often frames the game as DLC-priced content sold as a full title, or explicitly mentions feeling ripped off or requesting a refund.
in_scope: Explicit mention of price ($60, $70, 60€) as unjustified; Statements of regret over purchase; Comparisons to DLC pricing expectations; Calls to buy only on sale; Refund requests
out_of_scope: Short game length as standalone critique (no price mention) -> T01; Comparisons to DL1/DL2 scope without price mention -> T01
sample_quotes: "Right off the bat ill say that this game really should have stayed as DLC." [943 votes, 18h] | "Just DLC for 60€. Not worth 60€, 40€ is a much better price." [40 votes, 7h]
classification_note: Most robustly detected theme across all sampling strata (37% keyword match). Stable convergence. Apply when negative sentiment is primarily anchored to the purchase price. Can co-exist with T01.
keywords_indicative: price, $60, $70, 60€, not worth, refund, rip off, overpriced, DLC price, full price

name: World Design & Map Size
definition: Complaints about the physical size or variety of the game world: only one map where two was the series norm, a world that feels too small for the price/expectation, too much open wilderness with insufficient parkour-friendly urban areas, or repetitive environmental assets.
in_scope: World described as too small for a full-priced game; Expectation of two maps based on DL1/DL2 precedent; Open wilderness areas described as empty or uninteresting; Too few urban zones for parkour; Lack of environmental variety
out_of_scope: No fast travel making traversal tedious -> T12; World content (quests, activities) being sparse -> T01; Parkour movement design -> T17
sample_quotes: "For starters there's only one map, as opposed to the two maps that's become standard for the series. The rest is just wilderness." [943 votes, 18h]
classification_note: WARNING: Keyword rate (3%) severely underestimates true prevalence (~20%). Map size complaints use varied language: 'wilderness', 'empty', 'one area'. Classifier must detect paraphrases. Often co-occurs with T12 and T01.
keywords_indicative: small map, one map, tiny map, empty world, open wilderness, single map, map size, not enough urban, too much wilderness

name: Story & Narrative Quality
definition: Critique of the game's narrative execution: the main plot being predictable or clichéd, the villain (Baron) being under-developed, supporting characters being flat, fetch-quest structure dominating story missions, plot inconsistencies, or a weak/anti-climactic ending.
in_scope: Predictable or clichéd main plot; Villain (Baron) under-developed or not threatening; Flat/forgettable supporting characters; Story missions structured as boring fetch quests; Plot holes or inconsistencies; Weak/rushed/anti-climactic ending; NPC quality (dumb NPCs, lifeless world)
out_of_scope: Short story length as content-volume complaint -> T01; Boss fights being repetitive -> T08; Fetch quest structure as mission variety issue -> T09
sample_quotes: "The story is straightforward, lacking meaningful choices, with flat supporting characters and a weak antagonist." [68 votes, 21h] | "When you defeat the Hybrid Baron instead of some awesome beast-against-beast finale, we get a lame flashback." [453 votes, 25h]
classification_note: DIVERGES: Top-80 reviews = 40%, low-voted = 25%. Root cause: analytical reviewers use specific story critique vocabulary; lower-voted reviewers express dissatisfaction indirectly. Polarity scoping required — distinguish negative story critique from positive mention. True prevalence ~25%.
keywords_indicative: story is weak, plot, villain, baron, predictable, cliché, flat characters, weak ending, fetch quest, narrative, writing, NPC, boring story

name: Progression & Skill System
definition: Dissatisfaction with how player characters grow over time: removal of parkour XP, a skill tree perceived as sparse or filled with reused abilities, slow or unsatisfying levelling pace, or the progression loop feeling unrewarding.
in_scope: No XP awarded for parkour actions (regressed from DL1); Skill tree described as bare-bones, empty, or recycled; Slow XP/levelling pace; Beast skills unlockable too slowly; Forced skill path; Lack of meaningful build diversity
out_of_scope: Beast Mode mechanics (feel in combat) -> T06; Weapon upgrade/crafting economy -> T11; Difficulty curve from enemy scaling -> T14; Parkour movement design (feel, momentum) -> T17
sample_quotes: "XP gains for differing actions were CUT. You get XP for killing mobs and completing quests. NO MORE PARKOUR EXP." [824 votes, 2h] | "Skill tree — a joke. Just the same stuff from DL1, just locked now." [55 votes, 15h]
classification_note: Converges. T05=XP/levelling/skill-tree architecture. T06=Beast Mode feel. T17=parkour movement design. All three can co-occur.
keywords_indicative: skill tree, XP, parkour XP, no XP for parkour, progression, leveling, skill points, bare bones skills, reused skills

name: Beast Mode Mechanics
definition: Critique of Beast Mode as a gameplay mechanic: feeling shallow (primarily punching), becoming repetitive quickly, poor integration with combat variety, underpowered abilities relative to its narrative importance.
in_scope: Beast Mode described as one-dimensional (just punching/brawling); Beast abilities feeling gimmicky or underpowered; Mode becomes boring after initial novelty; Weak integration with story climax or final boss; Beast skill variety insufficient
out_of_scope: Beast skill tree unlocking being slow -> T05; Final boss as narrative letdown -> T04; Chimera boss fights being repetitive -> T08
sample_quotes: "Beast mode skills having different features other than punching stuff (mainly what you'll do)." [338 votes, 18h] | "Cool for the first hour but the beast mode skills are just whatever." [60 votes, 26h]
classification_note: Converges. Apply when reviewer addresses Beast Mode as a mechanic that underdelivers. Distinguish from T05 (unlock system) and T08 (Chimera boss fights). Can co-occur with T04 if narrative payoff disappointed.
keywords_indicative: beast mode, beast form, just punching, beast skills shallow, beast transform, beast abilities, beast mode boring

name: Night Cycle & Atmosphere
definition: Complaints about the night cycle failing to deliver the tension, incentive, or unique gameplay loop that defined DL1: insufficient rewards for venturing out at night, Dark Zones being equally accessible day and night, Volatile density that feels annoying rather than scary.
in_scope: Little or no incentive to go out at night; Dark Zones accessible and identical day/night; Volatiles too densely packed (annoyance over fear); Loss of DL1 night tension/stealth; Night as mandatory obstacle not compelling choice; 2x XP insufficient motivation
out_of_scope: Volatiles as combat difficulty -> T14; Technical bugs related to night -> T10; Dark Zone loot rewards being flat -> T11
sample_quotes: "There is no incentive to go out at night. Dark zones are the same day and night." [24 votes, 46h] | "Nights are harder but not scarier. The volatile spawn is so dense it takes away the scary factor." [24 votes, 46h]
classification_note: WARNING: Keyword rate (6%) underestimates true prevalence (~15%). Strong franchise-context theme, almost always references DL1 comparison. Night too difficult -> T14. No reason to go out at night -> T07.
keywords_indicative: no incentive at night, dark zones same day night, volatiles too many, night cycle boring, no reason to go at night, DL1 night better

name: Boss & Enemy Encounter Design
definition: Criticism of how enemy encounters and boss fights are designed: too few boss archetypes reused, boss fights lacking variety, repetitive kill animations, weak or anti-climactic final boss, or enemy AI being frustrating (dodge-spam, broken hitboxes).
in_scope: Only 2-3 boss types recycled; Boss fights requiring no strategy; Repetitive finishing animations; Final boss as mechanical/narrative letdown; Human enemy AI excessively dodging; Enemy hitboxes broken; Level-scaling making enemies spongy
out_of_scope: Combat system depth/weapon feel (non-boss) -> T14; Beast Mode attacks during boss fights -> T06; Bugs causing boss immortality -> T10
sample_quotes: "There are about 3 boss types that the game reuses throughout the story. I despise the bosses." [943 votes, 18h] | "Killing the Chimeras ends up being the same fights with no weaknesses — just hit it till it dies." [66 votes, 24h]
classification_note: Converges. Boss fights as narrative letdowns -> T04+T08 both. Bugs causing boss immortality -> T10 not T08.
keywords_indicative: boss types, boss fights boring, same boss, boss design, boss fight repetitive, final boss, enemy AI, hitboxes, enemies dodge too much, chimera fight

name: Mission Design & Activity Repetitiveness
definition: Dissatisfaction with how quests and open-world activities are structured: excessive fetch quests, point-A-to-B-to-A padding, Dark Zones lacking variety, the same mission template reused, or the open world feeling hollow.
in_scope: Story missions as fetch quests/running errands; Point A->B->A mission structure; Dark Zones as repetitive template-driven activities; Open world lacking unique encounters; Side quests generic or forgettable; Activity variety insufficient
out_of_scope: Game length being short overall -> T01; Story quality (writing, characters) -> T04; Boss fights being repetitive -> T08
sample_quotes: "A huge portion of this game's story is annoying (and boring) boss fights and fetch quests." [943 votes, 18h] | "The story is repetitive, framed around hunting chimeras and doing similar tasks for survivor groups." [195 votes, 24h]
classification_note: Converges. T09=HOW quests are designed (structure and variety). T01=how many there are. T04=how well the story is written.
keywords_indicative: repetitive quests, fetch quest, point A to B, same mission, same activity, dark zone repetitive, mission variety, copy paste quests

name: Technical Issues & Bugs
definition: Reports of technical malfunctions: game crashes, parkour movement bugs (missed ledge grabs), visual glitches (indoor rain, permanent day/night), co-op synchronisation issues, and quest-trigger bugs that prevent completion.
in_scope: Game crashes during/after cutscenes; Parkour ledge-grab failures (distinct from design critique); Visual bugs; Co-op state desynchronisation; Quest/interaction bugs blocking progress; Enemy immortality glitches; Performance drops/stutters
out_of_scope: Parkour momentum/design critique (not a malfunction) -> T17; Save system design complaints (intentional) -> T15; Co-op design/balance issues (not bugs) -> T13
sample_quotes: "Parkour is bugged. Sometimes Crane gets bugged during parkour and glitches, lets go, or just slides down a wall instead of grabbing the ledges." [453 votes, 25h] | "Random crashes during cutscenes. Raining indoors, permanent day/night visually." [104 votes, 22h]
classification_note: Converges strongly. KEY DISTINCTION vs T17: T10=parkour fails unexpectedly (ledge grab bug); T17=parkour consistently feels sluggish by design. Both may apply if reviewer conflates.
keywords_indicative: bug, crash, glitch, bugged parkour, ledge grab fail, stutter, FPS drop, visual bug, indoor rain, permanent night bug, quest bug, broken, freeze

name: Economy, Loot & Crafting
definition: Frustrations with the game's economic and resource systems: weapon durability degrading too quickly, loot rewards being uniform or unexciting, crafting materials scarce or limited to small set of farmable locations, or disconnect between farming effort and meaningful rewards.
in_scope: Weapon durability system seen as punishing or tedious; Loot drops described as flat/unrewarding; Limited Dark Zones (7) for resource farming; Crafting materials too scarce; In-game shops feeling expensive; No sense of economic progression
out_of_scope: Progression/XP system -> T05; Weapon variety/combat balance -> T14; Loot as incentive for night play -> T07
sample_quotes: "'Redirect attack' uses the same button as 'loot corpse,' so half my fights end up with Crane thinking +1 alcohol is more important than his own life." [183 votes, 19h]
classification_note: Converges. Weapon DURABILITY=T11; weapon VARIETY=T14. 'No reward for going out at night' can hold both T07 and T11.
keywords_indicative: weapon durability, loot is same, crafting materials, dark zone loot, weapon breaks too fast, economy, no reward, crafting system, repair

name: Navigation & Fast Travel
definition: Complaints about the absence of a fast travel system, forcing players to traverse large distances repeatedly. Includes vehicles as inadequate fast travel substitute.
in_scope: No fast travel system causing significant backtracking; Being forced to run the same routes repeatedly; Combination of large empty spaces + no fast travel; Vehicles as inadequate fast travel substitute
out_of_scope: Vehicle system as a game mechanic (slowness, handling) -> T16; Parkour movement design -> T17; Map being too small -> T03
sample_quotes: "The game doesn't have a fast travel which is just a massive pain in the butt." [453 votes, 25h] | "There is no fast travel, which would be fine if the world was compressed and fun to navigate." [195 votes, 24h]
classification_note: Converges. T12=no fast travel makes traversal tedious. T16=vehicle quality/feel. T03=world too small/empty. Can co-occur as T03+T12+T16.
keywords_indicative: no fast travel, fast travel missing, backtracking, run the same route, traversal tedious

name: Co-op & Multiplayer Experience
definition: Issues specific to the co-operative multiplayer experience: day/night state desynchronisation between players, level scaling that makes co-op sessions unbalanced, mandatory Epic Games account linking, and progress-blocking bugs only occurring in co-op.
in_scope: Day/night desync between co-op players; Level scaling creating extreme difficulty swings in co-op; Forced Epic Games account linking; Bugs uniquely occurring in co-op; Shared vs. independent progression clarity
out_of_scope: Technical bugs also present in single-player -> T10; Enemy difficulty balance -> T14; Epic account frustration as general UX -> T15
sample_quotes: "In co-op some players had daytime while others had night." [70 votes, 29h] | "Auto-scale is really noticeable in co-op — you are one-shotting everything or getting one-shot." [37 votes, 157h]
classification_note: Converges. Apply only to issues specifically multiplayer/co-op in nature. Co-op level scaling=T13+T14. Epic account frustration=T13+T15.
keywords_indicative: co-op, coop, multiplayer, day night desync, co-op bug, level scaling co-op, Epic account, online mode, join lobby

name: Difficulty & Combat Balance
definition: Concerns about difficulty scaling and combat mechanics balance: enemy level-scaling creating artificial sponge-like enemies, default difficulty feeling too easy, Brutal mode punishing through weapon durability rather than skill, or combat depth being too limited.
in_scope: Enemy level scaling described as artificial or unfun; Default difficulty perceived as too easy; Brutal mode relying on attrition rather than challenge; Combat described as too shallow (M1 spam + dodge); Zombie grapple mechanic cheap/unfair; Volatile density at night feeling punitive
out_of_scope: Combat bugs (hitboxes, glitches) -> T10; Boss design specifically -> T08; Weapon durability as economic system -> T11
sample_quotes: "Enemy level scaling — the worst design choice ever." [183 votes, 19h] | "More basic than DL2: M1, perfect dodge/parry, repeat." [121 votes, 18h]
classification_note: Converges. Often implicit — watch for proxies: weapon durability frustration, level scaling frustration, 'too easy' complaints. Can co-occur with T05, T08, T11, T13.
keywords_indicative: too easy, level scaling, difficulty, brutal mode, enemy sponge, combat too simple, M1 spam, zombie grapple, too hard, not challenging, balance

name: UI/UX & Accessibility
definition: Issues with user interface, control customisation, accessibility features, and save system design: inability to rebind all keys, insufficient accessibility options, a hybrid autosave/checkpoint system that loses progress unpredictably, or minor UX frictions.
in_scope: Incomplete keybinding/controller remapping options; Insufficient accessibility settings (text size, contrast); Autosave/checkpoint system losing progress frustratingly; Confusing or conflicting control bindings; UI elements cluttered or unclear; Forced external account requirements as UX friction
out_of_scope: Technical bugs in the UI (crashes, broken menus) -> T10; Epic Games forced linking as co-op issue -> T13; Skill tree design (too empty) -> T05
sample_quotes: "Not every function can be properly rebound from within the game, relying on external means or mods." [10 votes, 58h] | "Text sizes that are too small. Developers think a colorblind mode is enough when it's far from enough." [10 votes, 58h]
classification_note: Converges. High raw frequency partly noise. Validate that review specifically critiques UX/accessibility DESIGN, not just general game frustration.
keywords_indicative: keybinding, accessibility, text size, colorblind, autosave, save system, control remapping, conflicting buttons, save anywhere, UI, interface

name: Vehicle System
definition: NEW THEME (v2). Complaints about the vehicle/driving system: cars being significantly slower and more fragile than DL: The Following's buggy, vehicles feeling like a barebones or pump-and-dump travel tool, inability to rebind vehicle keys, no personalised vehicle, or vehicles failing as traversal substitutes across wilderness terrain.
in_scope: Cars described as slow, fragile, or made of cardboard; Barebones vehicle system vs. DL: The Following's buggy; Vehicles as pump-and-dump (use and discard without ownership); No personal vehicle; Vehicle key bindings not remappable; Driving in wilderness terrain described as useless or frustrating
out_of_scope: Vehicles as inadequate fast travel substitute -> T12; Inability to rebind vehicle keys (UX angle) -> T15; Vehicle physics bugs -> T10
sample_quotes: "The car sucks so bad. Unlike DL: The Following's buggy, these cars are slow and break so easily." [943 votes, 18h] | "The car system is just a barebones car with limited speed so traversing the map doesn't feel too fast." [453 votes, 25h] | "Yay cars are back, but they're slow, made of cardboard, and completely useless with all the trees in the way." [51 votes, 31h]
classification_note: NEW in v2 — discovered via cross-approach validation. Consistent across ALL sampling strata. 13% clearly negative vehicle sentiment in substantial reviews. Often co-occurs with T12. Apply T16 when critique is specifically about vehicle quality/feel, T12 when about the absence of fast travel system generally.
keywords_indicative: car sucks, car slow, car breaks, barebones car, vehicle system, vehicle handling, pump and dump, no personal vehicle, car useless, DL following buggy

name: Parkour & Movement Design
definition: NEW THEME (v2). Design critique of the parkour and movement system's feel, distinct from both technical bugs (T10) and the progression/XP system (T05): parkour lacking momentum compared to DL1/DL2, the grappling hook having poor targeting and feel, climbing speed feeling slow, or movement generally feeling floaty, sluggish, or less responsive than prior entries.
in_scope: Loss of momentum during parkour chains; Grappling hook targeting/feel described as poor or abysmal; Climbing speed feeling too slow; Movement described as float-y, clunky, or sluggish by design; Castor Woods poorly suited for parkour flow; Movement less responsive than DL1/DL2 by design
out_of_scope: Parkour ledge-grab failures (technical failure) -> T10; No XP for parkour actions -> T05; Limited urban parkour environments -> T03
sample_quotes: "There's nearly no momentum to everything you do. Extending jump with E after vaulting barely gives you any momentum." [453 votes, 25h] | "The grappling hook is abysmal, its targeting sucks, everything about it sucks. Climbing up is so slow." [60 votes, 26h] | "Parkour is pretty bad — a lot of actions kill momentum and grapple has issues that pause it mid-air removing flow." [37 votes, 157h]
classification_note: NEW in v2 — discovered via cross-approach validation. 16% of substantial reviews with design-specific language. KEY DISTINCTION TRIANGLE: T10=parkour breaks unexpectedly (bug); T17=parkour consistently feels wrong by design (momentum, grappling hook); T05=parkour offers no XP reward. All three can co-occur. Grappling hook is most consistent isolated complaint for T17.
keywords_indicative: momentum, grappling hook, movement feel, parkour feel, clunky movement, janky movement, movement is slow, climbing slow, float-y, sluggish movement, grapple issues, no momentum
